ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Aloo Zeera; A0CKP (potato-based dishes) used as the closest defensible broader category.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard side dish portion of Aloo Zeera is approximately 250 g, typically served alongside rice, roti, or dal as part of an Indian meal.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods — ICMR-NIN
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for Aloo Zeera are adapted from ICMR-NIN data for cooked potato preparations and cumin-spiced vegetable dishes. Core macros (energy 112 kcal, protein 2.2 g, fat 6.0 g, carbohydrate 12.0 g per 100 g) are consistent with ICMR-NIN values for sautéed potato dishes. Secondary nutrients (sodium 180 mg, dietary fibre 1.8 g, calcium 12 mg, iron 0.8 mg, vitamin C 10 mg) are also consistent with ICMR-NIN reference ranges for this dish type. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and several micronutrients are set to -1 where ICMR-NIN does not report detailed fatty acid or full micronutrient profiles for this preparation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson et al.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI value exists for Aloo Zeera as a composite dish. GI of 65 is estimated using boiled/cooked white potato (GI ~65, medium category) as the dominant carbohydrate source per the University of Sydney GI Database. The addition of fat (6 g/100 g) and fibre (1.8 g/100 g) from cumin, onion, and tomato may modestly lower the effective GI, but the potato base dominates. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 30.0) / 100 = 19.5 for a 250 g portion (30 g available carbohydrate).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 180 mg/100 g is below the 200 mg/100 g threshold; potassium from potato and onion provides a favourable DASH-diet counterbalance.
hypertension.guidance: Aloo Zeera is acceptable for hypertensive individuals at standard portions; request reduced added salt during cooking to keep sodium below 200 mg/100 g, and retain potato skins to maximise potassium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Potato is a medium-to-high GI carbohydrate (GI ~65); carbohydrate at 12 g/100 g with only 1.8 g/100 g dietary fibre results in a relatively rapid glucose response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 100–150 g, allow potatoes to cool before eating to increase resistant starch and lower effective GI, and pair with a high-fibre, high-protein accompaniment such as dal or raita to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat is low at 0.8 g/100 g; trans fat is minimal at 0.04 g/100 g; cholesterol is 0 mg/100 g; the dish is plant-based with no animal fat.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Aloo Zeera presents a low cardiovascular fat burden; use a heart-healthy oil (e.g., canola or olive oil) during sautéing, keep portion to 150–200 g, and pair with a fibre-rich accompaniment to support LDL management.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Potatoes, tomatoes, and green chilli are nightshade vegetables containing solanine, which may trigger joint inflammation in RA-sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with RA who are nightshade-sensitive should avoid or substitute potato with sweet potato and tomato with tamarind; cumin seeds have mild anti-inflammatory properties and may be retained.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Aloo Zeera (also spelled Aloo Jeera or Jeera Aloo) is a classic North Indian vegetarian side dish in which diced or cubed potatoes are sautéed in oil with whole cumin seeds (zeera/jeera), sliced onions, chopped green chillies, tomatoes, and fresh coriander. The dish is finished by simmering until the potatoes are tender and coated in a fragrant, lightly spiced masala. The flavour profile is earthy and aromatic from the cumin, mildly spicy from the chillies, and savoury from the tomato-onion base. The texture is soft potato with a slightly crisp exterior where the cumin seeds have toasted. Per 100 g, Aloo Zeera provides approximately 112 kcal, 2.2 g protein, 6.0 g fat (0.8 g saturated), 12.0 g carbohydrate, 1.8 g dietary fibre, and 180 mg sodium. It is naturally vegan, vegetarian, halal, and gluten-free. The dish has a medium glycemic index (~65) due to the potato base, making portion control important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium is below 200 mg/100 g, making it suitable for hypertensive individuals when prepared with reduced added salt. The low saturated fat and zero cholesterol profile make it cardiovascular-friendly. Aloo Zeera is commonly served as a side dish alongside roti, paratha, rice, or dal in Indian and Indian-Singaporean cuisine.
common_user_queries: Is Aloo Zeera good for diabetics?
What does Aloo Zeera taste like?
Is Aloo Zeera suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN reference data for cooked potato preparations, which is an approved primary source for Indian dishes. The dish is not listed as a named entry in ICMR-NIN but the macro profile is consistent with ICMR-NIN values for sautéed potato dishes. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using boiled white potato as the analogous food. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact named entry for Aloo Zeera exists in any single approved source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Aloo Zeera as a composite dish is not reported in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central for this specific preparation. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium is not reported for this dish in ICMR-NIN; estimated ~370 mg/100 g from potato and onion components but not confirmed by a single approved source entry and therefore set to -1 per numeric hygiene rules. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported for this specific composite dish preparation in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero — this is a fully plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero per ICMR-NIN and USDA FoodData Central for equivalent plant-based potato preparations. trans_fat_g is 0.04: trace level from cooking oil; retained as reported in source input. The ethnic_tag 'Butter Chicken' in the original input has been removed as it is not relevant to Aloo Zeera. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on boiled white potato from the University of Sydney GI Database; the effective GI of the composite dish may be slightly lower due to fat and fibre content.
